name: Triad Bank, National Association
description_short: Triad Bank is a community-focused bank serving Tulsa and surrounding Oklahoma areas since 1983, offering personal and business banking with mortgages, auto loans, and deposit accounts.
description_long: Triad Bank was founded in 1983 by local professionals with deep roots in the Tulsa area. The bank emphasizes its local orientation and community commitment, positioning itself as different from standard national banks. With over 30 employees, Triad has grown while maintaining focus on serving local individuals and small businesses rather than pursuing national expansion. The bank's philosophy centers on three core values: Commitment, Service, and Innovation—what they call "The Triad Way." Triad Bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business financial services. On the consumer side, they provide mortgage loans (home purchase, refinance, home equity, construction), auto loans, boat and RV financing, and personal banking accounts (checking and savings). For businesses, they offer commercial loans for equipment, inventory, working capital, real estate, and other fixed assets, plus business checking and savings accounts. Personal savings accounts require a minimum $100 deposit with quarterly variable-rate interest payments. The bank also provides online and mobile banking, payment processing, and financial calculators. Triad Bank distinguishes itself through explicit local market focus and personalized service. They market themselves as understanding the local area and prioritizing community members and businesses. The bank emphasizes affordable and fast mortgage processes tailored to individual borrowers' needs. Their business lending approach highlights support for local entrepreneurship and "out-of-the-box ideas." They maintain multiple physical locations and direct phone support (918-254-1444 and 1-800-317-6204), suggesting a relationship-based banking model rather than purely digital-first approach. Triad Bank is a legitimate community bank appropriate for consumers and small business owners in Oklahoma seeking personalized, local banking relationships. The main limitation is geographic—they specifically serve Tulsa and surrounding communities, making them inaccessible to customers outside their service area. Website content provides general service descriptions but lacks specific rate information, fee schedules, or detailed product terms. As a smaller regional bank, they may have different lending criteria or approval processes than national banks. Consumers should verify current rates and requirements before applying.
services: Home mortgage loans (purchase, refinance, home equity)
Personal savings accounts with quarterly interest
Online and mobile banking with secure access
pros: Local, community-focused bank founded in 1983 with established presence in Tulsa area
Comprehensive consumer lending including mortgages, auto, boat, and RV financing
Low minimum deposit requirement ($100) for savings accounts with quarterly interest payments
Dedicated business lending programs for equipment, working capital, inventory, and commercial real estate
Multiple contact methods including phone support and local branch locations
Transparent security practices with clear warnings about password protection
Online and mobile banking available with enrollment options
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Tulsa and surrounding Oklahoma communities
Website lacks specific interest rates, APR ranges, and detailed fee schedules for comparison
No information provided about minimum loan amounts, credit requirements, or approval timelines
Limited details on business loan terms, maximum amounts, or specific qualification criteria
Smaller institution may have less competitive rates or fewer product options than national banks
best_for: Tulsa-area residents seeking personalized mortgage and home financing with local service
Small business owners and entrepreneurs in Oklahoma wanting relationship-based commercial banking
Local consumers preferring community bank values over large national financial institutions
Borrowers seeking multiple financial products (checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages) from one local provider
diagnosis: Triad Bank is best for Tulsa-area residents and small business owners seeking personalized, community-focused banking with comprehensive lending products including mortgages and business loans. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank only serves Tulsa and surrounding Oklahoma communities, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community-focused Tulsa bank since 1983 offering personal and business banking with mortgages, auto loans, and deposit accounts.
